questions.question: What is the topic for Week 25?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Twenty-Five is STRENGTH IN QUIETNESS.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 24?
options: A. CHRISTIANS AND POLITICS: CHANGING THE NARRATIVES
B. CHRISTIANS AND POLITICS: BE INVOLVED
questions.explanation: Christians and Politics: Be Involved was the topic for Week 24.

questions.question: If anyone does not stumble in word, he is a perfect man, able also to bridle the whole ________.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (James 3:2) says 'able also to bridle the whole body.'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is James 3:2.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 25 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: For we all stumble in many things. If anyone does not stumble in word, he is a perfect man, able also to bridle the whole body.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of James 3:2.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (James 3:2), what does a person prove by not stumbling in word?
options: B. He is a perfect man, able to bridle the whole body
D. He is above the law
questions.explanation: The verse says 'If anyone does not stumble in word, he is a perfect man, able also to bridle the whole body.'

questions.question: What does the introduction say about being in a rush to speak?
options: A. It is a sign of intelligence
B. It is a sign of weakness and foolishness
C. It shows you have authority
D. It is necessary in business
questions.explanation: The text states 'Therefore, being in a rush to speak always is a sign of weakness and foolishness (James 1:19).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that words are powerful and can make or mar?
questions.explanation: The introduction says 'Words are powerful and can make or mar (Proverbs 6:2).'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'THE FOOL’S MOUTH'.

questions.question: According to Proverbs 18:7, what is a fool's mouth to him?
options: C. His destruction and a trap to his soul
questions.explanation: The text states 'According to Proverbs 18:7, a fool’s mouth is his destruction and his rash words are a trap to his soul.'

questions.question: A fool utters his entire mind and hates accountability.
questions.explanation: Point in Outline I says 'A fool utters his entire mind (Proverbs 29:11) and hates accountability.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that a fool's mouth is used to engage in ceaseless and unprofitable arguments?
questions.explanation: The text says 'A fool’s mouth is used to engage in ceaseless and unprofitable arguments (Proverbs 18:6)...'

questions.question: Why is a fool not trusted, according to Outline I?
options: A. Because he is always late
B. Because he lies all the time
C. Because of irregularity in speech
D. Because he steals from others
questions.explanation: The text says 'A fool is not trusted because of irregularity in speech (Proverbs 19:1).'

questions.question: Which verse is cited for a fool arguing, expressing frustrations openly, and thus cannot cease from strife?
questions.explanation: The text ends Outline I with 'A fool argues and expresses frustrations openly and thus cannot cease from strife (Proverbs 20:3).'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.answer: BEING SLOW TO SPEAK AND ITS BENEFITS
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'BEING SLOW TO SPEAK AND ITS BENEFITS'.

questions.question: According to the Week 25 lesson, being slow to speak is often a sign of weakness.
questions.explanation: Outline II explicitly states 'Being slow to speak is never a sign of weakness.'

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a step to be slow to speak?
options: B. Let our words be gracious
D. Ignore what others are saying
questions.explanation: Point 6 says 'Talk less... and listen actively and attentively to others', not ignore them.

questions.question: What should we ask the Lord to do for our mouth according to Psalm 141:3?
options: A. Set a guard at it
questions.explanation: Point 7 says 'Ask the Lord to set a guard at your mouth (Psalm 141:3; James 3:2).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support that we should avoid being rash with words and decisions?
questions.explanation: Point 4 cites '(Proverbs 15:1; Acts 19:36)'.

questions.question: According to the Week 25 lesson, if we 'bridle' our tongues, what will we command?
questions.explanation: The text states 'If we ‘bridle’ our tongues, we will command attention and respect (Proverbs 12:14).'

questions.question: By being slow to speak, we will avoid needless arguments, troubles, battles and keep safe.
questions.explanation: The text states 'We will avoid needless arguments, troubles, battles and keep safe (Proverbs 13:3; 21:23).'

questions.question: According to the conclusion, relationships grow by listening actively and speaking ________.
questions.explanation: The conclusion states: 'Relationships grow by listening actively and speaking slowly.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'In the multitude of words sin is not lacking, But he who restrains his lips is wise.'
questions.verse_text: In the multitude of words sin is not lacking, But he who restrains his lips is wise.
questions.explanation: This is verse 19 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (Proverbs 10:19), what is NOT lacking in the multitude of words?
questions.explanation: Verse 19 says 'In the multitude of words sin is not lacking...'

questions.question: According to Proverbs 10:20, what is the tongue of the righteous compared to?
questions.explanation: Verse 20 says 'The tongue of the righteous is choice silver...'

questions.question: According to Proverbs 10:20, what is the heart of the wicked worth?
options: D. It is full of evil
questions.explanation: Verse 20 says 'The heart of the wicked is worth little.'

questions.question: In Proverbs 10:21, the lips of the righteous feed many, but what happens to fools?
options: B. They die for lack of wisdom
questions.explanation: Verse 21 says 'But fools die for lack of wisdom.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that being slow to speak depicts understanding?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Also, it depicts understanding (Proverbs 10:13; 17:28b)...'

questions.question: The talk of a fool will destroy him because he does not take to instruction and rejects responsibilities.
questions.explanation: Outline I says 'The talk of a fool will destroy him because he does not take to instruction and rejects responsibilities (Ecclesiastes 10:12).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support that if we bridle our tongues, our words will be sound and seasoned?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Our words will be sound and seasoned (Titus 2:8; Proverbs 15:23).'
